key_questions: Inhaled corticosteroid use (most common cause — rinsing mouth after?)
red_flags: Unresponsive to antifungal → resistant Candida or alternative diagnosis

first_line.name: Nystatin Suspension
first_line.drugs: Nystatin 100,000 units/mL: 4–6 mL swish and swallow QID × 7–14 days
first_line.notes: Topical antifungal — swish vigorously throughout mouth for 2 min, then swallow. Effective and cheap. Continue 48h after symptoms resolve. Works by binding ergosterol in fungal cell membrane.
first_line.preferred_when: Mild-moderate thrush; first-line for most patients

first_line.name: Clotrimazole Troches
first_line.drugs: Clotrimazole 10 mg lozenge: dissolve slowly in mouth 5× daily × 14 days
first_line.notes: Good compliance. Dissolve slowly — do not chew. More convenient than nystatin for some patients.

second_line.name: Fluconazole (systemic)
second_line.drugs: Fluconazole 150 mg single dose OR 100 mg OD × 7–14 days
second_line.notes: For moderate-severe or refractory oral candidiasis, or denture-associated. Check drug interactions (many CYP3A4 interactions). Use if topical agents fail or patient unable to use topical.

non_pharm: ICS users: ALWAYS rinse mouth and gargle with water after each puffer use — prevent recurrence
Denture hygiene: remove dentures at night, soak in antifungal solution (nystatin) or dilute bleach
Good oral hygiene: soft toothbrush twice daily
Address predisposing factors (diabetes control, dry mouth treatment)
Spacer device for ICS — reduces oropharyngeal deposition
treatment.refer_when: Odynophagia/dysphagia (esophageal involvement); unresponsive to treatment; immunocompromised patients; recurrent thrush without clear cause
